stem: In a figure, two lines intersect at point C, forming triangle ABC. The measure of angle A is 45 degrees and the measure of angle B is 60 degrees. What is the measure of the angle that is vertical to angle ACB, in degrees?
rationale:
Choice C is correct. The angles of triangle ABC sum to 180 degrees, so the measure of angle ACB is 180 minus 45 minus 60 = 75 degrees. Vertical angles are congruent, so the angle vertical to angle ACB also measures 75 degrees.

Choice A is incorrect: 105 is the sum of 45 and 60.

Choice B is incorrect: 60 is the measure of angle B.

Choice D is incorrect: 180 is the sum of all three angles.
